summary: Broad mechanical decomp-matching PR generated by running the permuter briefly on high-percentage functions. It touched 35 files across fighter, item, interface, game-mode, effect, stage, library, menu, and toy code. The bot report credited 50 new matches, +6896 matched code bytes, and +20 matched data bytes. Most changes were compiler-shape nudges: explicit temporary variables, assignment expressions, comma operators, HSD_GObjGetUserData reloads instead of macros, inline helper wrappers, reordered stores/loads, no-op statements, and small header/formatting cleanups. The largest textual change was an approximately 8k-line removal from ftKb_Init.c of trailing Kirby function implementations; the PR body does not explain that deletion, so its rationale should be treated as inferred from the split Kirby SpecialN files and match report rather than explicit reviewer intent.

key_files.why: Largest change: +4/-7962. Matched ftKb_SpecialN_800EFF9C and ftKb_SpecialN_800F15D8 per bot report, added small HSD_GObjGetUserData/temp-variable match tweaks, and removed a very large trailing block of Kirby AttackDash/SpecialN/SpecialNGw functions. Bot also reported ftKb_Init .data improvement +1545 bytes and .sdata regression -23 bytes.

Kirby SpecialN matching tweaks for ftKb_SpecialHi3_Phys and fn_800F6588/fn_800F6638/fn_800F66E8/fn_800F6798, using comma operators, callback temporaries, HSD_GObjGetUserData reloads, and assignment expressions.
Kirby copy-ability SpecialN Pk/Kp/Lk/Ss fixes using explicit zero/NULL temps, a gobj alias before ftKb_SpecialNLk_UnsetBow, and HSD_GObjGetUserData.
Kirby Yoshi SpecialN fixes using chained assignments, HSD_GObjGetUserData reloads, and a documented comma-operator regalloc trick for take_dmg_cb.

Kirby Zelda/Sheik/Mewtwo/Ice Climbers/Purin SpecialN fixes, including HSD_GObjGetUserData, volatile/pad-style changes, temp constants, NULL masking, an inline pointer helper, and comma-operator comparisons.
Effect_RemoveUserData reached 100% by aliasing the void* argument, adding Effect_RemoveUserData_inline() to return &Effect_AllocData, and passing the helper result to HSD_ObjFree.
ftCo_80097F38 reached 100% by hoisting the DownDamageU ternary motion-state choice into an int temporary before Fighter_ChangeMotionState.

Multiple item matches: it_802E61C4, it_802E628C, and it_2E5A_UnkMotion1_Anim. Tactics included hoisting HSD_Randf, using *= form, introducing M_PI-derived temporaries, and loading velocity through an f32* temporary.
un_802FD9D8 reached 100% with a tiny no-op i++/i-- sequence after clearing slot state.
un_802FF6A0 and un_802FF710 reached 100% by hoisting HSD_LObjLoadDesc/HSD_CObjLoadDesc results into typed locals and using a literal 0x13 for the camera GObj class.
gmCamera_801A2798 reached 100% by hoisting x14 + 1 into an unsigned temporary before modulo by 2.
Mainly include-order, whitespace, ItemStateTable formatting, and prototype/style cleanup around Kyasarine egg item code; less evidence that these were semantic matching tactics.

what_changed: Ran the permuter on high-percentage functions, according to the PR body: "Ran permuter on all high % functions (only for 1 minute tbh), but this is what I got".
Changed 35 files with 202 added lines and 8100 deleted lines in the slice.
decomp-dev reported matched code 54.47%, +0.18%, +6896 bytes, and matched data 20.95%, +20 bytes.

decomp-dev reported 50 new matches, including un_802FD9D8, ftKb_SpecialN_800F15D8, un_80306C5C, gmCamera_801A2798, ftCo_80097F38, it_802E61C4, it_802E628C, Effect_RemoveUserData, ifcoget functions, soundtest functions, and several Kirby SpecialN helpers.

Removed a very large block of Kirby functions from ftKb_Init.c after ftKb_SpecialN_800F1F68. The slice shows deleted functions such as fn_800F1FDC, ftKb_AttackDash_Anim, Kirby SpecialHi/SpecialN stubs, and ftKb_SpecialNGw_8010D6D0; the reason is not stated in human text.

Adjusted Effect_RemoveUserData by adding Effect_RemoveUserData_inline(), aliasing the effect pointer through new_var/new_var2, preserving the duplicate-free OSReport/assert path, and calling HSD_ObjFree through the inline helper.
Adjusted ftCo_80097F38 by storing the conditional DownWait motion ID in a temporary before Fighter_ChangeMotionState.

Adjusted many Kirby SpecialN functions by replacing GET_FIGHTER reloads with HSD_GObjGetUserData in selected spots, hoisting callback pointers, using assignment expressions for NULL/zero/constants, and adding comma-operator expressions.
Adjusted item code in it_2E5A, it_2725, itclimbersstring, ithouou, itrshell, ittaru, and related headers with local temporaries, pointer aliases, and no-op checks to affect codegen.
Adjusted small interface/game/menu/library/stage/toy functions with low-risk expression-order changes, such as hoisting descriptor loads, global loads, pointer reloads, modulo inputs, and constants.
Included minor header and formatting changes in gm_1601.h, gmresult.h, grzakogenerator.static.h, itkyasarinegg.h, itkyasarinegg.c, it_266F.h, and itsamuschargeshot.c.
smart_moves: Targeting only high-percentage functions gave high ROI: many were already above 94-99% and were pushed to 100% with very small mechanical edits.
The PR kept most permuter changes local to individual functions, minimizing the semantic blast radius while still influencing register allocation and expression order.

Using HSD_GObjGetUserData directly instead of GET_FIGHTER/GET_ITEM in selected reloads was a practical way to alter macro expansion, casts, and register lifetimes without changing intended runtime behavior.

Hoisting expressions into locals before calls, especially motion IDs, callback pointers, object descriptors, random values, and constants, produced matches without restructuring the surrounding logic.
The inline helper around Effect_AllocData is a reusable pattern when the compiler needs a call-like or inline-returned address shape instead of a direct global address operand.
The bot report was used as objective evidence for a broad automatic patch: it showed 50 new matches and only one reported unmatched-item regression.
decomp_lessons: For near-matches, try the smallest possible compiler-shape perturbation first: a temporary variable, assignment expression, or call-argument hoist can be enough.
GET_FIGHTER(gobj) and HSD_GObjGetUserData(gobj) may be semantically equivalent in context but not codegen-equivalent; test both forms when reload timing or casts matter.

Comma operators such as (0, expr) and chained assignments such as msid = (new_var = 0x200) are accepted matching tools in this slice, but they should be isolated and ideally commented because they are not readability improvements.
No-op constructs can force instruction scheduling or register allocation: examples include do {} while (0), empty if blocks, i++ followed by i--, and redundant NULL/bitmask expressions.
Callback stores are sensitive to how function pointers are materialized; storing efLib_PauseAll/efLib_ResumeAll or item callbacks through temporaries can change the match.

Data and function-table matching can be affected by source organization. The ftKb_Init.c deletion coincided with a large .data improvement in the bot report, but the slice lacks an explicit explanation, so future work should verify file-split and duplicate-definition assumptions before reusing the tactic.

Automatic permuter output can be valuable even when ugly. Preserve the exact target function and bot match result so later cleanup can distinguish intentional codegen scaffolding from accidental slop.
Header/prototype cleanup should stay synchronized with implementation changes, but this PR provides little reviewer discussion about naming or API intent.
naming_conventions: Temporary variables from permuter output commonly appear as new_var and new_var2 in this PR; evidence suggests they are mechanical matching placeholders, not meaningful domain names.

Kirby fighter symbols use the ftKb_ prefix, with copy-ability or move-specific names such as ftKb_SpecialN_800F15D8, ftKb_SpecialNKp_800FA958, ftKb_ZdSpecialAirN_Anim, and ftKb_MtSpecialAirNCancel_Anim.
Common fighter symbols use ftCo_ prefixes, as in ftCo_80097F38 and ftCo_800CD278.
Item code uses Item_GObj*, Item*, it_802xxxxx address-style names, and item-specific prefixes such as itKyasarinegg_, itRShell_, and it_2E5A_.
Unknown interface/menu/toy functions retain un_ address-style names, such as un_802FD9D8, un_802FF6A0, un_803009E0, and un_80306C5C.
Header prototypes in this codebase often retain address comments; gmresult.h shows a small improvement from an unnamed parameter to struct ResultsPlayerData* data.
assembly_or_matching_tactics: Hoist a ternary result into an int local before a large function call, as in ftCo_80097F38 before Fighter_ChangeMotionState.
Create local aliases for object/userdata pointers to influence reloads and register allocation, as in Effect_RemoveUserData, it_80272828, and several ftKb SpecialN functions.
Replace selected GET_FIGHTER(gobj) reloads with HSD_GObjGetUserData(gobj), sometimes with explicit casts or void* intermediates.
Use assignment expressions to combine stores and values: msid = (new_var = 0x200), fp->fv.kb.xA4 = (new_var = NULL), new_var = (new_var2 = NULL), and fp->throw_flags_b0 = (new_var2 = 0).
Use comma operators to force expression shape: (0, fp->co_attrs.air_drift_max), (0, GET_FIGHTER(gobj)), (0, &item->x5D4_hitboxes[var_ctr].hit), and ((0, fp2->fv.kb.xC0)).
Hoist function pointers or callbacks through locals before assignment, including efLib_PauseAll, efLib_ResumeAll, ftCo_800BD1DC, and fn_802C28B8.

Wrap simple values in inline helper functions when direct operands do not match, such as Effect_RemoveUserData_inline() returning &Effect_AllocData and ftKb_MtSpecialAirNCancel_Anim_inline(arg0) returning arg0.
Use harmless no-op scaffolding when necessary for codegen: empty do/while, empty if, i++ then i--, redundant gobj checks, and volatile/pad locals.
Hoist descriptor loads before GObj object setup, as in ifcoget's HSD_LObjLoadDesc and HSD_CObjLoadDesc changes.
Hoist arithmetic inputs before final expression, such as gmCamera_80479BC8.gcus.x14 + 1 before % 2 and HSD_Randf before item damage/velocity calculations.
Reorder local declarations and assignments to match compiler register allocation, as seen in gm_8016ECE8, soundtest, and toy.c.

review_feedback.note: No human review comments or reviews were present in the slice.
Author stated the method and scope: ran permuter on high-percent functions for about one minute.
Reported GALE01 matched code 54.47% (+0.18%, +6896 bytes) and matched data 20.95% (+20 bytes).
Reported 50 new matches; the excerpt lists 30 and says there were 20 more.
Reported unmatched-item improvements for ftKb_Init .data (+1545 bytes, 77.52% to 88.94%) and it_2E5A .sdata2 (+11 bytes, 79.84% to 89.66%).
Reported one unmatched-item regression: ftKb_Init .sdata -23 bytes, 19.55% to 0.00%.

follow_up_queries: Find later PRs that clean up or rename new_var/new_var2 temporaries introduced by automatic permuter fixes.
Check whether the ftKb_Init .sdata regression from PR2200 was later recovered.
Search for other accepted HSD_GObjGetUserData versus GET_FIGHTER match fixes.
Search for other PRs using comma operator, empty if, do-while(0), or i++/i-- no-op tactics for regalloc.
Audit the large ftKb_Init.c deletion against split ftKb_SpecialN, ftKb_SpecialNPk, ftKb_SpecialNYs, and ftKb_SpecialNZd files before assuming this was purely duplicate-code cleanup.
Compare Effect_RemoveUserData_inline with other inline helpers used solely to materialize global allocator addresses.
Find decomp-dev reports around March 2026 mentioning ftKb_Init .data or it_2E5A .sdata2 improvements.
